question text: When an object is in a state of equilibrium, which of the following statements best describes the relationship between its masses?
answers: [{'answer_text': 'The mass is concentrated entirely at the top of the object.', 'text': 'The mass is concentrated entirely at the top of the object.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'The mass on the right side is double the mass on the left side.', 'text': 'The mass on the right side is double the mass on the left side.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'The object is constantly falling due to a lack of mass.', 'text': 'The object is constantly falling due to a lack of mass.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'The masses are fluctuating rapidly to create motion.', 'text': 'The masses are fluctuating rapidly to create motion.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'The mass has been removed to create a vacuum.', 'text': 'The mass has been removed to create a vacuum.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'The masses on each side of the center of gravity are equally matched.', 'text': 'The masses on each side of the center of gravity are equally matched.', 'weight': 100}]
a: The mass is concentrated entirely at the top of the object.
b: The mass on the right side is double the mass on the left side.
c: The object is constantly falling due to a lack of mass.
d: The masses are fluctuating rapidly to create motion.
e: The mass has been removed to create a vacuum.
f: The masses on each side of the center of gravity are equally matched.

question text: If a sculpture is leaning too far to the left, what is the most effective way to restore balance using a counterweight?
answers: [{'answer_text': 'Remove the base of the sculpture entirely.', 'text': 'Remove the base of the sculpture entirely.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'Add more weight to the left side to weigh it down further.', 'text': 'Add more weight to the left side to weigh it down further.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'Add an opposing weight or force to the right side to equalize the sides.', 'text': 'Add an opposing weight or force to the right side to equalize the sides.', 'weight': 100}, {'answer_text': 'Blow air on the left side to push it down.', 'text': 'Blow air on the left side to push it down.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'Heat up the left side to expand the materials.', 'text': 'Heat up the left side to expand the materials.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'Paint the right side a darker color to make it look heavier.', 'text': 'Paint the right side a darker color to make it look heavier.', 'weight': 0}]
a: Remove the base of the sculpture entirely.
b: Add more weight to the left side to weigh it down further.
c: Add an opposing weight or force to the right side to equalize the sides.
d: Blow air on the left side to push it down.
e: Heat up the left side to expand the materials.
f: Paint the right side a darker color to make it look heavier.

question text: In the context of stability, what usually happens when you lower an object’s center of gravity (for example, crouching down on a surfboard)?
answers: [{'answer_text': 'The object becomes more stable and harder to tip over.', 'text': 'The object becomes more stable and harder to tip over.', 'weight': 100}, {'answer_text': 'The object immediately falls over.', 'text': 'The object immediately falls over.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'The object becomes lighter in weight.', 'text': 'The object becomes lighter in weight.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'The object loses all friction with the ground.', 'text': 'The object loses all friction with the ground.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'The object begins to spin uncontrollably.', 'text': 'The object begins to spin uncontrollably.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'The object’s total mass doubles.', 'text': 'The object’s total mass doubles.', 'weight': 0}]
a: The object becomes more stable and harder to tip over.
c: The object becomes lighter in weight.
d: The object loses all friction with the ground.
e: The object begins to spin uncontrollably.

question text: What defines a "kinetic" system or sculpture compared to a static one?
answers: [{'answer_text': 'It is made of solid stone.', 'text': 'It is made of solid stone.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'It is painted in bright colors.', 'text': 'It is painted in bright colors.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'It is strictly two-dimensional.', 'text': 'It is strictly two-dimensional.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'It creates its own electricity.', 'text': 'It creates its own electricity.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'It involves movement or is a system in motion.', 'text': 'It involves movement or is a system in motion.', 'weight': 100}, {'answer_text': 'It is built underground.', 'text': 'It is built underground.', 'weight': 0}]
a: It is made of solid stone.

question text: According to the principles of forces, what happens when a force acting on an object is "unbalanced"?
answers: [{'answer_text': 'The object disappears.', 'text': 'The object disappears.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'It causes movement because the force is not met with an equal opposing force.', 'text': 'It causes movement because the force is not met with an equal opposing force.', 'weight': 100}, {'answer_text': 'The object enters a state of deep freezing.', 'text': 'The object enters a state of deep freezing.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'The object remains perfectly still.', 'text': 'The object remains perfectly still.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'The force turns into a liquid.', 'text': 'The force turns into a liquid.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'The object becomes weightless.', 'text': 'The object becomes weightless.', 'weight': 0}]
b: It causes movement because the force is not met with an equal opposing force.
c: The object enters a state of deep freezing.
e: The force turns into a liquid.

question text: If two people of different weights are trying to balance on a seesaw, how must they adjust their positions relative to the fulcrum?
answers: [{'answer_text': 'They must both sit as far back as possible.', 'text': 'They must both sit as far back as possible.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'They must stand on top of the fulcrum.', 'text': 'They must stand on top of the fulcrum.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'The lighter person must sit closer to the middle.', 'text': 'The lighter person must sit closer to the middle.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'The heavier person must sit closer to the fulcrum in the middle.', 'text': 'The heavier person must sit closer to the fulcrum in the middle.', 'weight': 100}, {'answer_text': 'The heavier person must jump up and down.', 'text': 'The heavier person must jump up and down.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'They must hold heavy rocks to match their weights perfectly.', 'text': 'They must hold heavy rocks to match their weights perfectly.', 'weight': 0}]
a: They must both sit as far back as possible.
b: They must stand on top of the fulcrum.
c: The lighter person must sit closer to the middle.
d: The heavier person must sit closer to the fulcrum in the middle.
e: The heavier person must jump up and down.
f: They must hold heavy rocks to match their weights perfectly.

question text: Why is the "Test" phase (such as using a fan) critical in the engineering design process for a balanced sculpture?
answers: [{'answer_text': 'To simulate environmental forces like wind to see if the design remains upright.', 'text': 'To simulate environmental forces like wind to see if the design remains upright.', 'weight': 100}, {'answer_text': 'To dry the glue faster.', 'text': 'To dry the glue faster.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'To blow away dust before presenting the project.', 'text': 'To blow away dust before presenting the project.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'To cool down the engineers after hard work.', 'text': 'To cool down the engineers after hard work.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'To see if the sculpture makes a musical noise.', 'text': 'To see if the sculpture makes a musical noise.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'To make the sculpture spin so fast it breaks.', 'text': 'To make the sculpture spin so fast it breaks.', 'weight': 0}]
a: To simulate environmental forces like wind to see if the design remains upright.
c: To blow away dust before presenting the project.
d: To cool down the engineers after hard work.
e: To see if the sculpture makes a musical noise.
f: To make the sculpture spin so fast it breaks.

question text: If a prototype fails to spin or falls over during testing, what is the logical next step in the design process?
answers: [{'answer_text': 'Throw the materials away and quit.', 'text': 'Throw the materials away and quit.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'Glue the sculpture to the table so it cannot move.', 'text': 'Glue the sculpture to the table so it cannot move.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'Blame the materials for being low quality.', 'text': 'Blame the materials for being low quality.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'Write a report saying it worked anyway.', 'text': 'Write a report saying it worked anyway.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'Evaluate the problem, plan a revision, and redesign the structure.', 'text': 'Evaluate the problem, plan a revision, and redesign the structure.', 'weight': 100}, {'answer_text': "Switch to a project that doesn't require physics.", 'text': "Switch to a project that doesn't require physics.", 'weight': 0}]
a: Throw the materials away and quit.
b: Glue the sculpture to the table so it cannot move.
c: Blame the materials for being low quality.
d: Write a report saying it worked anyway.
e: Evaluate the problem, plan a revision, and redesign the structure.
f: Switch to a project that doesn't require physics.

question text: Why do architects and engineers build scale models of skyscrapers before constructing the real buildings?
answers: [{'answer_text': 'To sell the models as toys.', 'text': 'To sell the models as toys.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'To practice painting small details.', 'text': 'To practice painting small details.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'Because they ran out of concrete for the real building.', 'text': 'Because they ran out of concrete for the real building.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'To test how forces like wind and gravity affect the structure’s stability and safety.', 'text': 'To test how forces like wind and gravity affect the structure’s stability and safety.', 'weight': 100}, {'answer_text': 'To see if the building will look good in a photograph.', 'text': 'To see if the building will look good in a photograph.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'To hide the blueprints from competitors.', 'text': 'To hide the blueprints from competitors.', 'weight': 0}]
a: To sell the models as toys.
c: Because they ran out of concrete for the real building.
d: To test how forces like wind and gravity affect the structure’s stability and safety.
e: To see if the building will look good in a photograph.
f: To hide the blueprints from competitors.

question text: In a STEM challenge regarding kinetic sculptures, which of the following would be a quantifiable "Success Criterion"?
answers: [{'answer_text': 'The sculpture looks very pretty.', 'text': 'The sculpture looks very pretty.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'The group had fun making it.', 'text': 'The group had fun making it.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'The sculpture remains upright and spins for 2 minutes in a breeze.', 'text': 'The sculpture remains upright and spins for 2 minutes in a breeze.', 'weight': 100}, {'answer_text': 'The sculpture uses a lot of tape.', 'text': 'The sculpture uses a lot of tape.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'The sculpture is taller than the teacher.', 'text': 'The sculpture is taller than the teacher.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'The sculpture is named after a famous scientist.', 'text': 'The sculpture is named after a famous scientist.', 'weight': 0}]
b: The group had fun making it.
